title: Hello, Kotlin!
description: Write your first Kotlin program and greet the world.

slides.title: Your First Program 🎬
slides.content:
## Tradition matters!

In the programming world, it is a tradition that your very first program should simply display the text **"Hello, World!"** on the screen.

Let's learn how to do that in Kotlin.

slides.title: Printing Text 🖨️
slides.content:
To show text on the screen in Kotlin, we use the `println()` command. (Pronounced 'print-line').

Whatever we want to print goes inside the parentheses `()` and is wrapped in double quotes `""`.

quizData.question: Which command is used to display text on the screen in Kotlin?
quizData.explanation: We use println() in Kotlin to display messages to the output console.

slides.title: Print vs Println
slides.content:
There is also a `print()` command. 

- `println()` prints the text and moves the cursor to the **next line**.
- `print()` just prints the text and stays on the **same line**.

quizData.question: What is the difference between `print()` and `println()`?

options.text: `println` is for numbers, `print` is for text.
`println` moves to a new line after printing, `print` does not.

quizData.explanation: `println()` automatically adds a line break (a new line) at the end of the message.
